{{Infobox concept
| name = Herald
| introduced = Wintersteel
}}

''See also: [[Herald Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A Herald is a powerful rank of being that exists above the level of [[Archlord]]. Achieving this status typically requires reaching the peak of the [[Archlord]] rank through brute force and a massive accumulation of resources. Once a person becomes a Herald, they advance to the rank of [[Monarch]]. Unlike [[Sages]], who focus their willpower externally to manipulate the world, [[Heralds]] direct their power inward to enhance their own capabilities and overcome limitations. This process results in a complete unification of the individual's spirit and physical body, allowing them to shift between spiritual and physical forms at will. This internal focus makes [[Heralds]] somewhat blind to [[Icons]], which [[Sages]] can interact with more easily. While [[Heralds]] possess immense strength and are invulnerable to forces lacking willpower, they remain susceptible to direct attacks. The transition into a Herald can be unstable if an individual advances too quickly or fails to balance their merged spirit and body, potentially resulting in a permanent state known as a pseudo-Herald. Historically, [[Heralds]] have been distinguished by wearing red badges, whereas [[Monarchs]] wear blue. Various individuals hold this title across different organizations, including the [[Redmoon Hall]], the [[Eight]]-Man [[Empire]], and the [[Abyssal Palace]].}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A Herald is a specific type of beast whose true nature remains a secret to anyone below the rank of [[Archlord]]. These entities, such as [[Xorrus]], possess bodies that are unified with their spirits and can shift between physical and spiritual forms. This unique power allows a Herald to pit the full strength of their will against an opponent. While [[Fury]] remains vigilant for their appearance, the threat of a Herald is significant, as seen when the [[dragon]]'s Herald had not yet descended from above to annihilate them. Furthermore, excessive killing of cultists carries the risk of drawing the attention of such an enemy.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">10</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Herald]] is a type of beast/entity.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The nature of [[Heralds]] is a secret for anyone below [[Archlord]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Herald]] can pit the full might of their spirit and will against another.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4">''Wintersteel'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Xorrus]] is a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Herald]]'s body is smaller than Sophara's head in her current form.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Killing too many cultists could draw the attention of the enemy [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c13">''Wintersteel'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Herald]] of the [[dragons]] had not yet annihilated them from a thousand feet up.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19">''Wintersteel'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Fury]] watches for [[Heralds]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24">''Wintersteel'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Herald]]'s spirit and body are one.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c26">''Wintersteel'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Herald]]'s power allows their body to shift between spiritual and physical forms.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c26" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Bloodline ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Mercy]] is associated with a tiny [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A stable [[Herald]] would not be affected as much by the suppression field as [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Being a [[Herald]] is described as a bumpy path full of sharp rocks.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Herald]] is a type of being that [[Lindon]] was uncertain how the [[Empty Palm]] would affect.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The rank of Herald is characterized by a unique relationship between the body and spirit, as seen in [[Yerin]]. Achieving this advancement allows [[Lindon]] to become a [[Monarch]], though it is widely believed that only those at the [[Sage]] or Herald level possess the power to survive and manipulate a [[labyrinth]]'s workings. [[Heralds]] exert significant spiritual pressure and can deploy techniques that obscure spiritual perception, such as the black-and-white copies of past experts. These entities, including [[Reigan Shen]], can move with such speed that they appear as a blur while weaving through projectiles. Furthermore, a Herald's [[Remnant]] is a specific type of [[Remnant]] capable of being tempered in [[Archlord soulfire]], and their spiritual presence can be immense, feeling like multiple [[Heralds]] stitched together.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">11</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|As a [[Herald]], [[Yerin]]'s body and spirit have a unique relationship.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Advancing to [[Herald]] will make [[Lindon]] a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|No one below [[Sage]] or [[Herald]] is believed to have the ability to survive and alter the [[labyrinth]]'s workings.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9">''Reaper'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]] is a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c11">''Reaper'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Herald]]'s [[Remnant]] is a type of [[Remnant]] that can be tempered in [[Archlord soulfire]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]] described the creature's spiritual presence as feeling like half a dozen [[Heralds]] stitched together.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c14">''Reaper'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Heralds]] can send out techniques that blot out spiritual perception.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19">''Reaper'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Herald]] possesses significant spiritual pressure.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20">''Reaper'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Herald]] can appear as a black-and-white copy of an expert from the past.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Herald]] is capable of weaving through arrows with speed that appears as a blur.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reigan Shen]] was a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c23">''Reaper'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A Herald is a specific stage of advancement and a member of the [[Eight]]-Man [[Empire]]. While [[Heralds]] are expected to possess greater physical strength than [[Sages]] and can travel via portals, [[Ziel]] viewed his own status as more of a Herald than a [[Sage]]. These beings possess bodies as tough as an [[Archlord]]'s and remain invulnerable to any forces lacking will, yet they can still be pushed back by [[Sages]]. During the conflict, a newborn Herald manifested on the deck of [[Redmoon Hall]], and a single Herald was seen walking into [[Malice]]'s strongholds. Additionally, the techniques used by the [[King's army]] are described as being collectively similar to a Herald.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">11</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Herald]] of the [[Eight]]-Man [[Empire]] is a member of that group.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Herald]] is a stage of advancement.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Ziel]] viewed himself as more of a [[Herald]] than a [[Sage]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Heralds]] can fly through portals.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Heralds]] are supposed to be physically stronger than [[Sages]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14">''Dreadgod'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Heralds]] are invulnerable to forces without will behind them.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Herald]]'s body is as tough as an [[Archlord]]'s.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Herald]] is being pushed back by the [[Sages]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16">''Dreadgod'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A newborn [[Herald]] appears on the deck of [[Redmoon Hall]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[King's army]] techniques are collectively like a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18">''Dreadgod'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Herald]] walked into [[Malice]]'s strongholds.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23">''Dreadgod'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The rank of Herald represents a significant advancement goal for [[Little Blue]] and a potential path for [[Mercy]]. Achieving this state requires spirits to form themselves into a physical body, a process aided by the [[Twin Dragon Rejuvenation Pill]] which helps Archlords synchronize their spirit and body. While the [[Path of Seven Pages]] prepares a spirit for this transition, the seventh page of the Book is specifically meant to be mastered once one has reached the rank. This stage of power is formidable, as evidenced by [[Yerin]] killing a Herald and the general difficulty involved in capturing an average one. During certain events, [[Orthos]] and [[Little Blue]] emerged as a new [[Sage]] and Herald respectively.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Little Blue]]'s eventual advancement goal is to reach [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Herald]] is a potential path or rank for [[Mercy]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Path of Seven Pages]] prepares the spirit for [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c7">''Waybound'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Twin Dragon Rejuvenation Pill]] helps Archlords synchronize spirit and body to bring them closer to [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Mercy]] can borrow the power of a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c15">''Waybound'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Yerin]] killed a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c16">''Waybound'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Spirits reach [[Herald]] by forming their spirits into a body.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c17">''Waybound'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Orthos]] and [[Little Blue]] were a new [[Sage]] and [[Herald]] respectively.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19">''Waybound'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Herald]]'s body is a type of physical state.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23">''Waybound'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The seventh page of the Book is meant to be mastered when one is a [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c25">''Waybound'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The energy in the page is meant to take one to [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c25" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|It is hard to capture an average [[Herald]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c26">''Waybound'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|“Last chance? You think they’ll haul you back from spending time with the youngest Sage and some kind of tiny Herald?<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“I can’t see anyone below Sage or Herald having the ability to both survive and alter its workings,” Eithan said.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“Not a peak Archlord. And I had always seen myself as more of a Herald than a Sage anyway.”<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle concepts]]
[[Category:Lifeforms]]
